name: Natural Park of Las Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja — Circular from Torrevieja
name_original: Parque Natural de Las Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja — Circular desde Torrevieja
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Torrevieja offer diverse terrain, from flat coastal paths to more challenging inland hills. The region is characterized by its unique salt lakes, including the pink Laguna Rosa, and extensive natural parks with wetlands. Cyclists can find routes along the Mediterranean coast, through protected natural areas, and towards reservoirs and low mountain ranges. This varied landscape provides options for different road cycling preferences. Best road cycling routes around Torrevieja The most popular road cycling route is Natural Park of Las Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja — Circular from Torrevieja, a 65.7 miles (105.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 14 minutes to complete. This route explores the natural park, passing by the salt lakes and wetlands. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Conesa Castle – Rebate Climb (CV-952) loop from Torrevieja, a moderate 45.0 miles (72.4 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain, leading towards Conesa Castle and the Rebate Climb. Local road cyclists also love the La Pedrera Reservoir – La Pedrera Reservoir loop from Torrevieja, a 36.4 miles (58.6 km) trail leading through the area around La Pedrera Reservoir, often completed in about 3 hours 48 minutes. Road cycling around Torrevieja is defined by its salt lakes, coastal paths, and gentle hills leading to reservoirs and natural parks. The network offers options for various ability levels, from flat rides to routes with notable elevation gains. The routes in Torrevieja are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 120 reviews. More than 5000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Torrevieja's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Torrevieja?
A: There are over 100 road cycling routes around Torrevieja, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These include routes along the coast, through natural parks, and towards reservoirs and low mountain ranges.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Torrevieja?
A: Torrevieja's road cycling routes feature diverse terrain. You'll find flat coastal paths, routes alongside unique salt lakes, and gentle hills leading to reservoirs. For more challenging rides, routes extend into nearby mountains with significant elevation gains.

Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Torrevieja?
A: Yes, Torrevieja offers many easy and beginner-friendly road cycling routes. The region's flat coastal paths and the 'Via Verde' (greenway) are ideal for a relaxed ride. The greenway, which follows an old railway line, is traffic-free and perfect for those seeking a peaceful experience.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes around Torrevieja?
A: For a more challenging experience, consider routes that venture into the mountains. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38300919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Conesa Castle – Rebate Climb (CV-952) loop from Torrevieja&lt;/a&gt; offers significant elevation gain, leading towards Conesa Castle and the Rebate Climb. Other routes in the vicinity can reach nearly 700 meters of elevation.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in the Torrevieja area?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Torrevieja are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e890950910/natural-park-of-las-lagunas-de-la-mata-y-torrevieja-circular-from-torrevieja" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Park of Las Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja — Circular from Torrevieja&lt;/a&gt; is a popular loop exploring the natural park and salt lakes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39561172" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Pedrera Reservoir – La Pedrera Reservoir loop from Torrevieja&lt;/a&gt; also offers a scenic circular ride.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: You can explore unique natural features like the stunning pink &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/864166" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mata Lagoon&lt;/a&gt; and the Natural Park of Las Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja. Routes also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3508487" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riegos de Levante Canal&lt;/a&gt; and offer views of the Mediterranean coast. For historical interest, some routes lead to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5128264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de la Horadada Watchtower&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3507318" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Quarries of Playa del Río&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints on the road cycling routes near Torrevieja?
A: Absolutely. The routes along the coastline, such as the Ronda Litoral, offer beautiful views of the beaches and waterfront. Rides towards the Sierra de Callosa de Segura provide impressive limestone mountain backdrops. The areas around the salt lakes and natural parks also offer picturesque vistas, especially during sunrise or sunset.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Torrevieja?
A: Torrevieja is an excellent destination for road cycling year-round, thanks to its mild Mediterranean climate. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for longer rides. Even in winter, the weather is generally favorable, making it a popular choice for cyclists looking to escape colder climates.

Q: Is it possible to find parking near the road cycling routes in Torrevieja?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Torrevieja, especially near popular starting points for cycling routes. Many routes begin from urban areas or natural parks where designated parking facilities can be found. It's advisable to check specific route details for parking recommendations.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Torrevieja?
A: The road cycling routes in Torrevieja are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Reviewers often praise the diverse terrain, from flat coastal rides to challenging mountain climbs, and the unique natural beauty of the salt lakes and natural parks. The well-maintained paths and variety of options for all skill levels are also frequently highlighted.

Q: Are there any routes suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, the region is very family-friendly for cycling. The 'Via Verde' (greenway) is a safe, traffic-free path that follows an old railway line, circling the pink salt lake and connecting to other routes. This makes it perfect for families or those seeking a gentle, peaceful ride away from traffic.

Q: Can I combine road cycling with birdwatching in Torrevieja?
A: Definitely. The Natural Park of Las Lagunas de La Mata y Torrevieja is a significant natural space with numerous bike paths through wetlands and salt pans. It's an excellent spot for birdwatching, with opportunities to see various species, including pink flamingos. The park features dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5589759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birdwatching Huts&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there routes that pass by the famous salt lakes?
A: Yes, Torrevieja is renowned for its salt lakes, and several cycling routes offer stunning views of them. The Torrevieja Greenway, for instance, provides peaceful cycling paths alongside the striking pink Laguna Rosa and the blue-green salt lakes, offering a unique natural spectacle.

Q: Where can I find routes with coffee stops or places to rest?
A: Many routes in Torrevieja pass through or near towns and villages where you can find cafes, restaurants, and shops for refreshments. Coastal routes often have numerous establishments along the promenade. For routes venturing inland, it's a good idea to check the map for villages or designated picnic areas along the way.

Q: Are there routes that offer significant elevation gain for a more strenuous ride?
A: For cyclists seeking significant elevation gain, routes extending into the nearby mountains are ideal. The ride towards the Sierra de Callosa de Segura, for example, presents a winding road with an ascent to 132 meters. Other routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38300919" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Conesa Castle – Rebate Climb (CV-952) loop&lt;/a&gt;, can involve nearly 700 meters of elevation, providing a challenging workout.
